mnemonics.title: Prim's Process
mnemonics.content: PRIM: Pick edges by selecting minimum-weight edges that connect unvisited vertices to your growing tree. Remember: PRopagate, Record, Incrementally build tree by Choosing Minimum crossing edge.

mnemonics.title: MST Key Properties
mnemonics.content: MST = Minimum Spanning Tree: Must have (1) Exactly V-1 edges, (2) No cycles, (3) Connects all vertices, (4) Minimum total weight

acronyms.usage: A spanning tree with minimum total edge weight among all spanning trees of the graph
The collection of MSTs for each connected component in a disconnected graph

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a line dividing visited vertices (left) from unvisited vertices (right). The 'cut' is the boundary. Prim's always picks the minimum weight edge crossing this boundary - like a bridge connecting the two sides.

Think of the MST as a tree starting from a seed vertex (root). At each step, it grows one new branch - the cheapest branch possible that reaches an unvisited vertex. This is like a plant growing along the ground, always taking the shortest path to new territory.

analogies.analogy: Like building a road network: at each step, build the cheapest road that connects a new city to your existing road network. You never reconsider - you just keep extending from where you are.

Think of cutting a cake into two pieces. The cheapest slice that goes through the cut (connects the two halves) is always part of some optimal cutting strategy - you can't do better without using at least one of those cheap edges.
